Dryopteris filix-mas, or the male fern, is a type of fern native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The male fern has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and menstrual disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of the male fern include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and other phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from the male fern include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
